title: Korean-Style Fried Minari with Uruguayan Spices
description: A crispy fried Minari dish with a unique blend of Korean and Uruguayan flavors.

ingredients.name: Minari
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh Minari for best results

ingredients.name: Uruguayan Chimichurri
ingredients.notes: Available at most Latin American markets

ingredients.name: Gochujang
ingredients.notes: Korean chili paste for added depth

instructions: 1. Wash the Minari thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels.
2. In a shallow bowl, mix together cornstarch, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
3. Dip each Minari piece into the cornstarch mixture, shaking off excess.
4. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
5. When oil reaches 350°F (175°C), add a few Minari pieces to the skillet.
6. Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
7. Remove Minari from oil and place on paper towels to drain excess oil.
8. In a small bowl, whisk together Uruguayan Chimichurri and gochujang.
9. Toss the fried Minari in the chimichurri-gochujang mixture until well coated.
